methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found the UAM Support for Teaching AI page, the UAM guide news page, and official research/news results that were checked for scope.

methodsAttempted.query: UAM Guía visual sobre el uso de la Inteligencia Artificial para docentes y estudiantes
methodsAttempted.notes: The UAM Support for Teaching page links directly to the official PDF guide; the PDF was fetched by direct HTTP and converted to text locally because Firecrawl PDF OCR was noisy.
Spanish-language query found an official 2025 training notice and confirmed the strongest public evidence remained the UAM guide and support page.

artifacts.summary: Official UAM discovery found a public UAM Support for Teaching AI page and its linked guide, 'Guía básica sobre el uso de la Inteligencia Artificial para docentes y estudiantes'. The staged claims are limited to the guide's recommendations. Official research/news and training pages were rejected for broad policy claims because they are not policy or operational guidance sources.

artifacts.claimText: UAM's guide frames generative AI recommendations as suggestions that do not replace the capacity, creativity, or autonomous work of teachers and students, and are not sufficient by themselves for class preparation or assessment tasks.
artifacts.normalizedValue: generative AI is supplemental and not a substitute for autonomous work

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guía básica sobre el uso de la Inteligencia Artificial para docentes y estudiantes
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Los usos y recomendaciones que hacemos a continuación son sugerencias que en ningún caso pueden sustituir la capacidad, creatividad y trabajo autónomo de docentes y estudiantes, ni constituir en sí mismas prácticas suficientes para la preparación de clases o para completar pruebas de evaluación.

artifacts.title: Universidad Autónoma de Madrid generative AI guidance staged crawl
artifacts.summary: Staged extraction found an official UAM guide for teachers and students on appropriate use of generative AI in university education. Claims are limited to the guide's recommendation wording: generative AI is framed as supplemental, teachers are encouraged to specify permitted tools for graded work, students should reference assistive technologies used, and students are advised not to provide personal or sensitive information to generative AI tools.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The main source is an official UAM guide written as recommendations and suggestions; staged claims do not treat it as a binding university-wide policy unless the source uses that framing.
Official research/news and training pages discovered during the crawl were rejected for broad policy claims because they did not state current UAM AI rules.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
